title: 10 Córdobas

obverse.description:
Colour: Green and light blue. 
Fortress of the Immaculate Conception on southern bank of the San Juan River in El Castillo (Castillo Inmaculada Concepción).
Issuer name on top left, face value in numbers on top left and lower right corners, in letters below issuer name. Serial #s 8 digits with prefix letter over number, horizontal red on top center, vertical black on left edge; green signature with name and title below, on lower center. Two round see-thru windows with small image of the fortress on left, with embossed "10" on upper right. Raised circle device for visually impaired on top right. Creation detail on lower right.
obverse.lettering:
10           BANCO CENTRAL DE NICARAGUA
DIEZ CÓRDOBAS
A/1 05339625
CASTILLO INMACULADA
CONCEPCIÓN         10
ANTENOR ROSALES BOLAÑOS
PRESIDENTE
BANCO CENTRAL DE NICARAGUA
RESOLUCIÓN DEL CONSEJO DIRECTIVO DEL BANCO CENTRAL DE NICARAGUA CD-BCN-XXXVI-1-07 DEL12 DE SEPTIEMBRE DE 2007
obverse.lettering_translation:
10           Central Bank of Nicaragua
Ten Córdobas
Immaculate Conception castle
Antenor Rosales Bolaños, President, Central Bank of Nicaragua
Resolution of the Directors Board of Central Bank of Nicaragua # CD-BCN-XXXVI-1-07 from September 12th., 2007

reverse.description:
Nicaraguan coat of arms; adobe farm house with tiled roof on site of National War battle in 1856 (Casa Hacienda San Jacinto in Managua). Issuer name on top, face value in numbers on top right and left edge; in letters on lower left.
Simulated security thread.
reverse.lettering:
BANCO CENTRAL DE NICARAGUA         10
10
DIEZ CÓRDOBAS
REPÚBLICA DE NICARAGUA
- AMÉRICA CENTRAL -
HACIENDA SAN JACINTO
reverse.lettering_translation:
Central Bank of Nicaragua 10
Ten Córdobas
Republic of Nicaragua, Central America
"San Jacinto" farm

comments:
See-thru window (as watermark)

There are two varieties: 

          

             Embossed “10”                                                White “10”

                   © ngdawa                                                   © adanieluy

They are easy to see, but difficult to make pictures, so was needed to add color background to make them visible.

 

UV activity:

© gyoschak

 

On the P#201b (version with the white 10) there is a blue iridescent ink strip on the reverse. It has “10” repeated on it:

© Brandon Bertolli

watermark.description: Embossed "10", with "10" numbers repeated several times around. (see comments for variants)
